chunks.text: My sister has been continuing her practice for the marathon every day.
In two more weeks she will run the race.
she was too exhausted to run ten kilometers and would end up walking halfway.
Her ability could not catch up with her goal.
But she made effort after effort and gradually learned how to control her breathing.
Lately she has enough sufficient power to finish the full ten kilometers.
I go out to the bank of the river to watch her.
In order to reach her goal, she runs every day.
To have such a clear purpose is something bright and admirable.
I am sure that on the race day
she will cross the finish line with a smile.
On that day she will no longer be only an athlete but a mature person who has given her own pursuit a real shape.
